name: Brownie Cakes in a Jar
recipeInstructions:
1. Sterilize two 1‑pint straight-sided wide-mouth canning jars by boiling them with lids and rings for 10 minutes. Keep the jars in hot water until ready to use; set aside.
2. In a small bowl, stir together flour, sugar, baking soda, and cinnamon (if using).
3. In a medium saucepan, combine butter or margarine, water, and cocoa powder. Heat and stir until butter is melted and mixture is well blended. Remove from heat.
4. Stir in the dry ingredient mixture. Add buttermilk, egg, and vanilla; beat by hand until smooth.
5. Stir in chopped walnuts. Pour the batter into the prepared jars.
6. Place jars onto a cookie sheet. Preheat oven to 325 °F (165 °C).
7. Bake for 35–40 minutes or until a pick inserted deep into each cake comes out clean.
8. Remove cakes from the oven and let cool completely on a counter.
9. Once cooled, place lids on the jars and screw down tightly. Use heavy-duty mitts when handling hot jars; they will remain hot.
10. If you hear a “plinking” sound when pressing the lid, it is sealed. Store the cakes in a cool, dark place for up to one year. For decoration, add a wad of cotton to the jar lid, then cover with decorative cloth (cut larger than the lid). Secure the ring and decorate as desired.
11. These make wonderful Christmas gifts!
